userRequest: Create implementation plan for issue #637: hooks-mux: add HTTP, MCP tool, prompt, and agent handler types.

include: Extend hooks-mux HandlerRef with a backward-compatible type model where omitted type remains command.
Dispatch hooks-mux handlers by type from the normalizer runner instead of unconditionally executing shell commands.
Add HTTP, MCP tool, prompt, and agent handler modules with typed configuration and UnifiedHookResult-compatible output.
Preserve legacy command handler behavior, diagnostics, timeout handling, fail-open/fail-closed policy, plan ordering, and public imports.
Wire typed hook handler configuration through agent-platform/agent-mux bridge surfaces where needed.
exclude: Do not implement the missing hook events from #636 except to consume stable landed interfaces.
Do not implement defer/block/retry decision work from #638 except to remain compatible with landed result types.
Do not create a second MCP tool registry; use or adapt to the existing #576/tool-mux direction.
Do not add real external service dependencies to tests.

handlerTypes.notes: Existing shell subprocess behavior remains the compatibility baseline.

handlerTypes.expectedBehavior: POST normalized event JSON, parse JSON UnifiedHookResult response, enforce timeout and allowedEnvVars header interpolation.
Invoke an MCP tool through the existing registry/tool-mux path or a narrow injectable seam if #576 is not landed.
Evaluate a bounded model prompt using existing platform invocation surfaces, parse UnifiedHookResult-compatible output, and enforce timeout/cancellation.
Spawn or route to an agent responder through existing agent-platform/agent-mux surfaces with maxTurns/depth controls.

qualityGates: Reuse audit is rendered before implementation work and identifies existing command, bridge, and MCP registry infrastructure.
Dependency readiness for #636 and #576 is assessed before coding; ambiguous interface choices raise a maintainer breakpoint.
Runtime trace lists live call paths from handler registration through dispatch, merge, and bridge forwarding before implementation.
HandlerRef remains backward compatible: omitted type behaves as command and existing command tests still pass.
Each handler type has contract tests before implementation, with no real external services required.
HTTP handler tests cover URL validation, allowedEnvVars-only header interpolation, timeout, result parsing, and secret-leak prevention.
mcp_tool uses the existing registry/tool-mux path or a clearly documented narrow compatibility seam, not a duplicate registry.
prompt and agent handlers enforce timeout/cancellation and bounded recursion or max-turn controls.
Unsupported handler types and handler failures interact correctly with fail-open/fail-closed policy.
Documentation reflects the implemented support and any residual dependency-limited gaps.

parallelDependencyPolicy.issue636: Proceed only against stable landed handler/event dispatch interfaces. If #636 is not landed or changes core runner contracts incompatibly, raise the dependency breakpoint.
parallelDependencyPolicy.issue576: Use the unified tool registry/tool-mux path for mcp_tool. If unavailable, implement only a narrow injectable executor seam with tests and document the temporary assumption.
parallelDependencyPolicy.issue638: Do not implement decision semantics here, but keep result parsing compatible with any landed defer/block/retry result types.
